name: Vitthalanatha

dates_evidence.description: Mughal-period court references to his community-leadership.

core_thesis: Viṭṭhalanātha is the consolidator of *śuddhādvaita* and the institutional architect of *Puṣṭi-Mārga*. He preserves Vallabha's doctrine without alteration — Brahman-as-Kṛṣṇa as the sole reality, the world and *jīvas* as real *aṃśas* through *avirbhāva-tirobhāva* without *māyā*, the rejection of *jagat-mithyātva*, *Puṣṭi* (divine grace) as the operative soteriological principle — and supplies what his father did not live to complete: the second half of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras* and the unfinished portions of the *Subodhinī* on the *Bhāgavata*. Constructively, his *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* defends the school against rival Vedānta polemic, particularly Advaita. His larger contribution is institutional: the establishment of the seven *gaddīs* (seats) under his sons, the codification of *seva* as *aṣṭa-yāma* (eightfold daily Deity-service in śṛṅgāra register), and the formation of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* as a stable lineage with characteristic temple practice, *kīrtana* tradition, and a developed *bhakta*-typology distinguishing *puṣṭi*, *maryādā*, and *pravāha* souls.

engaged_works.title: Vidvan-Mandana
engaged_works.summary: The *Vidvan-Maṇḍana* ("adornment of the learned") is Viṭṭhalanātha's polemical defence of *śuddhādvaita* against rival Vedānta schools, principally post-Śaṅkara Advaita. Working topic by topic — *pramāṇa*, the nature of *Brahman*, the status of the world, the *jīva*'s ontology, *bandha* and *mokṣa*, *bhakti* — the work stages the *pūrvapakṣa* with technical care and answers it from the *śuddhādvaita* settled position. Its central polemical thrust is the rejection of *māyā-vāda* and *jagat-mithyātva*: where Advaita holds the world to be *vivarta* (illusory appearance) of Brahman, Viṭṭhalanātha defends Vallabha's *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda*, on which the world is a real, unmodifying transformation of Brahman's own *sat-cit-ānanda*. The text also clarifies the *jīva*'s status as *aṃśa* with *ānanda* concealed — a position requiring careful demarcation from Viśiṣṭādvaita's *aṃśa*-doctrine. It is the school's mature scholastic instrument and the standard Puṣṭimārga reference in inter-Vedāntic dispute.

engaged_works.title: Completion of Anu-Bhashya (third pada onward)
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored continuation of Vallabha's Aṇu-Bhāṣya from the third pāda.
engaged_works.summary: Viṭṭhalanātha's continuation of the *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* takes up Vallabha's incomplete commentary roughly from the third *pāda* of the third *adhyāya* and carries it through the fourth *adhyāya* to completion. The continuation maintains the terse glossing style and doctrinal commitments of Vallabha's portion: *avikṛta-pariṇāma-vāda*, the rejection of *māyā-vāda*, the *aṃśa* status of *jīva* and *jagat*, *bhakti* as the operative path. Substantively, the continuation engages the *adhyāyas* concerned with *sādhana*, the conditions and stages of *mokṣa*, and the post-mortem state of the liberated — material in which Viṭṭhalanātha's *Puṣṭi-Mārga* commitments emerge clearly: liberation is bestowed by Bhagavān's *anugraha*, not earned by *sādhana* mechanically; the liberated state is participation in Kṛṣṇa's *līlā* through eternal *seva*, not absorption. The completion makes the school's Brahma-Sūtra commentary functionally available and is the canonical Puṣṣmārga reading of the closing *adhyāyas*.

engaged_works.title: Subodhini completion
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored continuation of Vallabha's Subodhinī.
engaged_works.summary: Viṭṭhalanātha's *Subodhinī*-continuation supplies the books and passages of the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* that Vallabha did not live to gloss in his verse-by-verse commentary. The continuation preserves the father's exegetical method and *śuddhādvaita* doctrinal commitments, and is doctrinally indistinguishable in tone, but it permits a fuller view of *Puṣṭi-Mārga* devotional theology since Viṭṭhalanātha is also developing the *seva*-tradition in his own right. Specially consequential are passages on the eleventh book — the *Uddhava-Gītā* and the doctrines of *bhakti* and renunciation — and on the structures of devotion that Viṭṭhalanātha's institutional codification will then translate into temple-practice. Together with Vallabha's portion, the completed *Subodhinī* is the principal Puṣṭimārga *Bhāgavata*-commentary and the textual centre of the lineage's contemplative life.
